---
title: ""
---

```{=html}
<div style="text-align: center; padding: 3rem 1rem 2rem;">
  <h1 style="font-size: 2.2rem; font-weight: 700; color: #1F4E79;">
    Limma DEG Interactive Explorer
  </h1>
  <p style="font-size: 1.15rem; color: #555; max-width: 680px; margin: 1rem auto;">
    Interactive web applications for differential gene expression analysis
    using limma-voom, implemented in both R and Python Shiny.
  </p>
</div>
```

## About

This resource accompanies the manuscript:

> **[PAPER TITLE]**  
> Agudelo-Romero et al., [Year]  
> *[Journal Name]*. DOI: [DOI]

The paper describes ten practical rules for building reproducible, interactive
data analysis applications in the life sciences. Two parallel implementations
of the same Limma DEG Explorer app — one in R Shiny and one in Python Shiny —
serve as the worked example throughout the manuscript.

Both applications accept a CSV file produced by `limma::topTable()` and
provide real-time interactive visualisation of:

- Differentially expressed genes (volcano plot, ranked bar plot, DEG table)
- Gene Ontology enrichment (dot plot + table)
- Downloadable results folder (HTML report, CSV, Excel)

The validation dataset is GSE60450 (mouse mammary gland luminal vs basal
cells; [Fu et al. 2015](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GSE60450)).

---

## Launch the apps

```{=html}
<div style="display: flex; gap: 2rem; flex-wrap: wrap; justify-content: center; margin: 2.5rem 0;">

  <!-- R App -->
  <div style="
    flex: 1; min-width: 260px; max-width: 340px;
    border: 1px solid #dee2e6; border-radius: 12px;
    padding: 2rem 1.5rem; text-align: center;
    background: #f8f9fa;
  ">
    <div style="font-size: 2.5rem; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">📊</div>
    <h3 style="color: #1F4E79; margin: 0 0 0.5rem;">R Shiny</h3>
    <p style="color: #666; font-size: 0.9rem; min-height: 60px;">
      Built with R Shiny, ggplot2, limma-voom, clusterProfiler,
      and org.Mm.eg.db. Runs fully offline.
    </p>
    <a href="https://YOUR_USERNAME.shinyapps.io/limma-deg-explorer-r/"
       target="_blank"
       style="
         display: inline-block; margin-top: 1rem;
         background: #2E74B5; color: white;
         padding: 0.6rem 1.4rem; border-radius: 6px;
         text-decoration: none; font-weight: 600;
       ">
      Open R App →
    </a>
  </div>

  <!-- Python App -->
  <div style="
    flex: 1; min-width: 260px; max-width: 340px;
    border: 1px solid #dee2e6; border-radius: 12px;
    padding: 2rem 1.5rem; text-align: center;
    background: #f8f9fa;
  ">
    <div style="font-size: 2.5rem; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">🐍</div>
    <h3 style="color: #1E8449; margin: 0 0 0.5rem;">Python Shiny</h3>
    <p style="color: #666; font-size: 0.9rem; min-height: 60px;">
      Built with Python Shiny, matplotlib, scipy, gseapy (Enrichr API),
      and Jinja2. Requires internet for GO enrichment.
    </p>
    <a href="https://huggingface.co/spaces/YOUR_USERNAME/limma-deg-explorer-py"
       target="_blank"
       style="
         display: inline-block; margin-top: 1rem;
         background: #1E8449; color: white;
         padding: 0.6rem 1.4rem; border-radius: 6px;
         text-decoration: none; font-weight: 600;
       ">
      Open Python App →
    </a>
  </div>

</div>
```

---

## Key differences between implementations

| Feature | R | Python |
|---|---|---|
| Framework | R Shiny | Python Shiny |
| Plotting | ggplot2 | matplotlib |
| Gene labels | ggrepel | adjustText |
| DEA method | limma-voom | t-test (approx.) |
| GO enrichment | clusterProfiler | gseapy (Enrichr API) |
| Gene annotation | org.Mm.eg.db (offline) | mygene API (online) |
| Report engine | R Markdown | Jinja2 + base64 |
| Internet required | No | Yes (GO enrichment) |

---

## Reproduce the analysis

All code is available on GitHub. To run either app locally:

**R:**
```r
# Install dependencies
source("R/00_setup.R")

# Download and prepare data
source("R/01_data_prep.R")

# Launch app
shiny::runApp("R/")
```

**Python:**
```bash
# Create environment and install dependencies
python Python/00_setup.py

# Activate environment
conda activate shiny_app_py

# Download and prepare data
python Python/01_data_prep.py

# Launch app
shiny run Python/app.py
```

---

## Citation

If you use these applications in your research, please cite:

> Agudelo-Romero P. *et al.* **[PAPER TITLE]**. *[Journal]* [Year]; DOI: [DOI]

---

## Acknowledgements

Patricia Agudelo-Romero · The Kids Research Institute Australia  
[patricia.agudeloromero@thekids.org.au](mailto:patricia.agudeloromero@thekids.org.au)
Senior Research Innovation Fellow | [Future Health Research and Innovation Fund](https://fhrifund.health.wa.gov.au/News-and-Events/2025/05/07/Pioneering-WA-health-and-medical-innovators-supported)

Validation dataset: Fu *et al.* 2015, *Nature Cell Biology*.
GEO accession [GSE60450](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GSE60450).
